entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The host plant used in the study to evaluate midge resistance and parasitization.

entities.name: STENODIPLOSIS SORGHICOLA
entities.description: The sorghum midge, a pest that infests sorghum genotypes.

entities.name: APROSTOCETUS SPP
entities.description: Parasitoids that target the larvae of the sorghum midge.

entities.name: PATANCHERU
entities.description: The location in Andhra Pradesh, India, where the research was conducted.

entities.name: MIDGE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of certain sorghum genotypes to resist infestation by Stenodiplosis sorghicola.

entities.name: MIDGE SUSCEPTIBILITY
entities.description: The lack of resistance to Stenodiplosis sorghicola in certain sorghum genotypes.

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The seasonal period (1992/93) during which studies were conducted.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The seasonal period (1993) during which studies were conducted.

entities.name: APROSTOCETUS COIMBATORENSIS
entities.description: A specific parasitoid species predominant during the post-rainy season.

entities.name: APROSTOCETUS GALA
entities.description: A specific parasitoid species predominant during the rainy season.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL CONTROL
entities.description: The use of parasitoids to manage and reduce sorghum midge populations.

entities.name: PARASITIZATION LEVEL
entities.description: The degree to which midge larvae are parasitized by Aprostocetus spp.

entities.name: MIDGE INFESTATION
entities.description: The occurrence and density of sorghum midge on the host plant.

entities.name: HOST PLANT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The genetic resistance of the sorghum plant to the midge pest.

entities.name: PEST MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The integrated approach using host plant resistance and biological control to manage pests.
